title: ACER Decision No 06-2025 on the Lithuanian-Swedish cross-zonal hedging product decision deadline extension request
text:
DECISION No 06/2025
OF THE EUROPEAN UNION AGENCY
FOR THE COOPERATION OF ENERGY REGULATORS
of 11 August 2025
on the request of the regulatory authorities of Lithuania and Sweden to
extend the period for coordinated decisions on the proposed necessary
arrangements on a cross-zonal hedging product for the Lithuanian-
Swedish bidding zone border
THE EUROPEAN UNION AGENCY FOR THE COOPERATION OF ENERGY
REGULATORS,
Having regard to the Treaty on the Functioning of the European Union,
Having regard to Regulation (EU) 2019/942 of the European Parliament and of the Council of
5 June 2019 establishing a European Union Agency for the Cooperation of Energy Regulators1,
and, in particular, Article 6(10) thereof,
Having regard to Commission Regulation (EU) 2016/1719 of 26 September 2016 establishing
a guideline on forward capacity allocation2, and, in particular, Article 30(6) thereof,
Having regard to the outcome of the consultation with ACER’s Electricity Working Group,
Having regard to the favourable opinion of the Board of Regulators of 6 August 2025, delivered
pursuant to Article 22(5)(a) of Regulation (EU) 2019/942,
Whereas:
1. INTRODUCTION
(1) Commission Regulation (EU) 2016/1719 (the ‘FCA Regulation’) lays down detailed
rules on cross-zonal capacity allocation in the forward markets. One of the key
objectives of the FCA Regulation, specified in its Article 3, is the promotion of
1 OJ L 158, 14.6.2019, p. 22.
2 OJ L 259, 27.9.2016, p. 42.
Page 1 of 6

Decision No 06/2025
effective long-term cross-zonal trade with long-term cross-zonal hedging
opportunities for market participants.
(2) Article 30 of the FCA Regulation sets out a process whereby the competent regulatory
authorities decide on cross-zonal risk hedging opportunities.
(3) The present Decision follows from the joint request of the regulatory authority of
Lithuania, National Energy Regulatory Council (NERC), and the regulatory authority
of Sweden, Energy Markets Inspectorate (Ei), to extend the period for reaching
coordinated decisions on the joint proposal submitted by the Swedish transmission
system operator, Affärsverket Svenska kraftnät (the Swedish TSO) and the Lithuanian
transmission system operator, Litgrid AB (the Lithuanian TSO), for making long-term
cross-zonal hedging products other than long-term transmission rights (LTTRs)
available in accordance with Article 30(6) of the FCA Regulation. The requested
extension is for six months, i.e. until 12 November 2025, pursuant to Article 6(10),
subparagraph three, of Regulation (EU) 2019/942.
2. PROCEDURE
(4) By letter dated 9 May 2025, NERC, also on behalf of Ei, submitted to ACER a joint
request to grant a six-month extension, i.e. until 12 November 2025, according to
Article 6(10) of Regulation (EU) 2019/942, for reaching a coordinated decision on a
joint proposal submitted by the Lithuanian and the Swedish TSO in accordance with
Article 30(6) of the FCA Regulation.
(5) According to this letter, following Ei’s and NERC’s assessments of cross-zonal risk
hedging opportunities for the Lithuanian-Swedish bidding zone border in line with
Article 30(3) and (4) of the FCA Regulation, their finding that the risk hedging
opportunities are insufficient in the Lithuanian bidding zone, and their coordinated
decisions under Article 30(5)(b) of the FCA Regulation requesting the Lithuanian and
the Swedish TSO to propose a hedging product, those TSOs submitted a proposal for
an Electricity Price Area Differentials Vilnius (‘EPAD’) product for approval under
Article 30(6) of the FCA Regulation, on 12 November 2024.
(6) In support of the extension request, the letter explains that on 15 January 2025 Ei
proposed to NERC to agree to approve the arrangement for the hedging product prosed
by the Lithuanian and the Swedish TSO, while NERC considers that uncertainties
remain in the TSO´s proposal regarding the costs and future benefits for final
consumers and associated risks of implementing such hedging products in the market
and that more time is necessary to evaluate the potential participation of the Lithuanian
TSO in the secondary market for hedging instruments.
(7) Specifically, it is stated that:
• ‘NERC is not ready to approve the EPAD Vilnius proposal in its current form, due
to the high uncertainty related to costs and benefits of implementation of hedging
opportunities. NERC intends to continue consultations with stakeholders in order
Page 2 of 6

Decision No 06/2025
to develop an optimal hedging product for the LT-SE4 cross-border
interconnector.’
• ‘Ei is ready to move forward with the TSO´s proposed arrangement but respects
that NERC requires additional time to assess the consequences and concerns
outlined in this letter.’
• ‘NERC and Ei take the position that additional time is needed to analyse the
questions related to the implementation of EPAD combo hedging product on the
LT-SE4 cross-border interconnector. Allowing for additional time could facilitate
a well-coordinated and informed decision’.
(8) On 15 July 2025, a proposed draft of the present decision was submitted to ACER’s
Electricity Working Group for consultation in accordance with Article 24(2) of
Regulation (EU) 2019/942.
(9) On 22 July 2025, ACER’s Electricity Working Group endorsed the draft ACER
Decision on the request of Ei and NERC to extend the period for them reaching a
coordinated decision on the necessary arrangements on cross-zonal risk hedging
opportunities until 12 November 2025.
(10) On 6 August 2025, ACER’s Board of Regulators issued a favourable opinion pursuant
to Article 22(5)(a) of Regulation (EU) 2019/942.
3. ASSESSMENT OF THE REQUEST
Legal framework
(11) According to point (a) of the second subparagraph of Article 6(10) of Regulation (EU)
2019/942, ACER shall decide on regulatory issues having effects on cross-border
trade or cross-border system security, which require a joint decision by at least two
regulatory authorities, where such competences have been conferred on the regulatory
authorities under network codes and guidelines adopted before 4 July 2019 and
subsequent revisions of those network codes and guidelines and where the competent
national regulatory authorities have not been able to reach an agreement within a
period of six months from the referral of the case to the last of those regulatory
authorities.
(12) According to the third subparagraph of Article 6(10) of Regulation (EU) 2019/942,
the competent national regulatory authorities may jointly request that the six-month
period is extended by a period of up to six months.
(13) According to Article 30(5) of the FCA Regulation (which has been adopted as a
guideline before 4 July 2019), in case insufficient cross-zonal risk hedging
opportunities are identified in one or more bidding zones, the competent regulatory
authorities of the bidding zone border shall request the relevant TSOs:
a) to issue LTTRs; or
Page 3 of 6

Decision No 06/2025
b) to make sure that other long-term cross-zonal hedging products are made
available to support the functioning of wholesale electricity markets.
(14) According to Article 30(6) of the FCA Regulation, in case the competent regulatory
authorities choose to issue a request as referred to in Article 30(5)(b) of the FCA
Regulation, namely to request the relevant TSOs to make sure that other long-term
cross-zonal hedging products are made available to support the functioning of
wholesale electricity markets, those TSOs shall develop the necessary arrangements
and submit them to the competent regulatory authorities' approval no later than six
months after the request by the competent regulatory authorities.
Admissibility
(15) Ei and NERC requested an extension of the six-month period according to Article
6(10), subparagraph three, of Regulation (EU) 2019/942 to take coordinated decisions
on the necessary arrangements on a cross-zonal hedging product proposed by the
Lithuanian and the Swedish TSO for the Lithuanian-Swedish bidding zone border, in
accordance with Article 30(6) of the FCA Regulation.
(16) Article 30(6) of the FCA Regulation requires the competent regulatory authorities that
requested the relevant TSOs to make sure that other long-term cross-zonal hedging
products are made available according to Article 30(5)(b) of the FCA Regulation, to
approve the necessary arrangements developed and submitted by those TSOs.
(17) Arrangements for a cross-zonal hedging product have effects on cross-border trade,
and the requirement of their approval by the regulatory authorities indicates a
regulatory issue. Where the relevant TSOs submit the necessary arrangements for the
regulatory authorities’ approval under Article 30(6) of the FCA Regulation as a joint
proposal, the regulatory authorities need to coordinate their decisions and reach an
agreement and, effectively, a joint decision. Accordingly, the approval of such
arrangements falls within the scope of Article 6(10) of Regulation (EU) 2019/942 and
consequently the six-month period for the regulatory authorities to reach an agreement
under point (a) of the second subparagraph of that provision can be subject to an
extension under the third paragraph of the same provision.
(18) For an extension of this six-month period, the joint request by the competent
regulatory authorities should be submitted before the end of the period for reaching
an agreement and coordinated decisions, and the maximum requested extension
should not exceed the period of six months in accordance with Article 6(10),
subparagraph three, of Regulation (EU) 2019/942.
(19) The approval of the necessary arrangements on a cross-zonal hedging product to
which the present request for extension relates, falls under the competence of Ei and
NERC pursuant to Article 30(6) of the FCA Regulation. Accordingly, those regulatory
authorities are also the competent regulatory authorities that may jointly request an
extension of the six-month period.
Page 4 of 6

Decision No 06/2025
(20) In that regard it is to note that the present request for extension was submitted to ACER
by NERC, for both NERC and Ei, as their joint request.
(21) Ei and NERC received the TSOs’ a proposal for a hedging product in line with
Article 30(5)(b) of the FCA Regulation for approval under Article 30(6) of the same
regulation on 12 November 2024. Therefore, Ei and NERC were required to approve
the submitted proposal on the cross-zonal hedging product within six months from 12
November 2024, i.e. by 12 May 2025. The request for extension was received by
ACER on 9 May 2025, and hence before the expiry of the six-month deadline.
(22) In its request, Ei and NERC ask for an extension of six months, which, given the initial
deadline of 12 May 2025, would defer the final deadline to 12 November 2025. As
such, the requested extension does not exceed the maximum limit of six months as
provided for in Article 6(10), subparagraph three, of Regulation (EU) 2019/942.
(23) In light of the above, ACER considers the request for extension admissible.
Substance
(24) Article 6(10), subparagraph three, of Regulation (EU) 2019/942 does not lay down
requirements for the justification of an extension.
(25) The justification provided by Ei and NERC, as reported at paragraphs (5) to (7) above,
indicates the need for more time to analyse the questions related to the implementation
of the EPAD product on the LT-SE4 interconnector in order for the regulatory
authorities to be able to reach coordinated decisions on the arrangements proposed by
the TSOs.
(26) ACER considers that continuing and completing the regulatory authorities’ ongoing
analyse can contribute to the efficient conclusion of the pending proceedings. In
ACER’s view, this continuation and completion would not unduly impact the
fulfilment of the objective of the FCA Regulation. Under those circumstances, ACER
deems it reasonable to enable the regulatory authorities to conclude the proceedings
to reach coordinated decisions.
(27) Furthermore, ACER considers that the request for extension for a total of six months
is proportionate in light of the activities to be performed by the competent regulatory
authorities.
(28) Therefore, ACER considers an extension until 12 November 2025 justified.
4. CONCLUSION
(29) For the above reasons, ACER accepts the request for an extension submitted by the
regulatory authorities of Lithuania and Sweden and extends, pursuant to Article 6(10),
subparagraph three, of Regulation (EU) 2019/942, the period for those regulatory
authorities to reach coordinated decisions on the proposed necessary arrangements for
Page 5 of 6

Decision No 06/2025
the cross-zonal hedging product for the Lithuanian-Swedish bidding zone border, until
12 November 2025.
HAS ADOPTED THIS DECISION:
Article 1
The period within which the regulatory authorities of Lithuania and Sweden shall reach
coordinated decisions on the necessary arrangements for the cross-zonal risk hedging product
proposed by the Lithuanian and the Swedish TSO for the Lithuanian-Swedish bidding zone
border, is extended, in accordance with Article 6(10) of Regulation (EU) 2019/942, by six
months, until 12 November 2025.
Article 2
This Decision is addressed to National Energy Regulatory Council (Lithuania) and Energy
Markets Inspectorate (Sweden).
Done at Ljubljana, on 11 August 2025.
Fоr the Agency
The Director
C. ZINGLERSEN
In accordance with Article 28 of Regulation (EU) 2019/942, the addressee may
appeal against this Decision by filing an appeal, together with the statement of
grounds, in writing at the Board of Appeal of the Agency within two months of the
day of notification of this Decision.
In accordance with Article 29 of Regulation (EU) 2019/942, the addressee may
bring an action for the annulment before the Court of Justice only after the
exhaustion of the appeal procedure referred to in Article 28 of that Regulation.
Page 6 of 6
